# cuesheet-pipeline

> Vision-first rough-cut generation for dialogue-free footage — a cuesheet JSON contract, local
> ffmpeg render, and natural-language editing via MCP.

Point it at a folder of raw footage with no dialogue (e.g. a knitting vlog) and it produces a
rough-cut cuesheet by having a vision model judge extracted frames, then lets a human or an
agent refine that cuesheet (browser editor, or natural-language commands through an MCP bridge)
before rendering the final video locally with ffmpeg. The cuesheet (JSON) is the single source
of truth every step reads from or writes to.

## Docs

- [README](./README.md): quickstart, what it does, why it exists.
- [AGENTS.md](./AGENTS.md): onboarding for an agent operating this tool on a user's behalf —
  exact CLI invocations, the MCP bridge tool surface, file/path conventions, typical workflows.
- [ARCHITECTURE.md](docs/ARCHITECTURE.md): package map, data flow, dependency graph, key design
  decisions, for anyone developing the repo itself (paired with CLAUDE.md).
- [docs/PRD.md](./docs/PRD.md): product requirements — north star, user scenario, feature list.
- [docs/screen-spec.md](./docs/screen-spec.md): canonical screen layout for the web editor.
- [GitHub wiki](https://github.com/let-sunny/cuesheet-pipeline/wiki): experiment write-ups —
  editing-grammar reverse-engineering, scene-detection measurement (and why it was rejected),
  rough-cut pipeline iterations.
